hypothesis: Enhancing steric complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions by introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will increase selectivity margin over off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining strong SSTR2 binding.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is valid, but the focus must shift from maximizing ddG to optimizing selectivity. This requires targeted mutation of non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt off-target interactions.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are likely involved in off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt conserved-pocket contacts while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
Switching to a selectivity-weighted objective will prioritize candidates with higher Δmargin, ensuring that selectivity is the primary optimization goal.
